questions.question: Write C functions to insert a node at the beginning, end, and at a given position in a singly linked list. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Insert at beginning: Create node, set next to head, update head. O(1).
Insert at end: Create node, traverse to last, set last->next to new node. O(n).
Insert at position: Traverse to (pos-1), link new node to next, update previous link. O(n).
Handle edge cases: empty list, invalid position, position 1 (same as beginning).
Use malloc for dynamic allocation and check for allocation failure.

questions.question: Explain the deletion operations on a singly linked list with C code. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Delete from beginning: temp = head, head = head->next, free(temp). O(1).
Delete from end: Traverse to second-to-last, free last node, set next to NULL. O(n).
Delete by value: Find node with matching data, unlink it from chain, free it. O(n).
Always check for empty list before deletion.

questions.question: Write a C function to reverse a singly linked list without creating new nodes. Explain the algorithm step by step. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Use three pointers: prev (NULL), current (head), next.
Loop: next = current->next, current->next = prev, prev = current, current = next.
After loop, set head = prev (new first node).
Time: O(n), Space: O(1) - in-place reversal.

questions.question: Write the time complexity of each linked list operation and explain why. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Insert at beginning: O(1) - only pointer updates, no traversal.
Insert at end / Delete from end: O(n) - must traverse entire list.
Search / Display: O(n) - must visit each node.
Delete from beginning: O(1) - only head pointer update.
